authority_lanes.definition: The numbered printed card in one canonical English physical set.
authority_lanes.required_gate: canonical set and printed identity proven
notes: Must not multiply because a source exposes finishes or price buckets.
Missing-card backfill may only create this identity after separate approval.

authority_lanes.definition: A physical finish or edition treatment of the same canonical card identity.
notes: Should not become separate checklist cards.
Requires source agreement or reviewable source conflict evidence.

authority_lanes.definition: A collector-recognized stamped or event/product marked version of a canonical card identity.
notes: Needs stronger provenance than generic finish signals.
May require product or checklist policy review.

authority_lanes.definition: A marketplace, price, SKU, or product-feed distinction that may not be a collector-print variant.
notes: Must not become collector variant authority by default.
Can remain useful pricing evidence after classification.

authority_lanes.definition: A source-local term or payload signal that has not been mapped to normalized authority vocabulary.
notes: Preserve raw strings and payload references.
Translate only through an approved authority vocabulary.

do_not_backfill_yet: TCGPlayer price keys as collector variants
PokemonTCG API price buckets as canonical variants
TCGdex variant signals in unresolved hard-stop set groups
variants for cards with null or disputed printed numbers
stamped variants without product or checklist evidence
variant rows during missing card checklist backfill

future_schema_responsibilities.purpose: Store approved variant rows keyed to canonical card_print identity.
future_schema_responsibilities.write_prerequisite: canonical set, printed number, and active identity proven

future_schema_responsibilities.purpose: Store raw source terms, source ids, and payload references without mutating canonical authority.
Map finish, edition, stamp, product, and market-only terms to approved meanings.
Capture source disagreement without collapsing conflicting terms into a canonical row.
future_schema_responsibilities.write_prerequisite: conflict lane and review process approved

hard_stop_gates: set canonicalization hard stops excluded or resolved
canonical target set exists and owns the card identity
active identity/domain conflicts are zero for candidate scope
candidate is not part of simultaneous missing-card backfill
source terms map to approved vocabulary
post-write verification proves canonical card counts unchanged
next_no_write_queue: classify collector, stamped, market-only, product-only, and unknown lanes
produce future dry-run implementation plan only after contract approval
